resources.string: CyberApp
Skill Pre-Test
Question 1 - Which of these is the strongest password?
MichaelFL05
password0
S3cur3_616
Question 2 - What should you do if you get a suspicious email from someone you do not know?
Open it and click all the links
Delete it or mark it as spam
Reply to ask who they are
Question 3 - John is using his work laptop in a public cafe and has to step away to grab a drink, what should he do before leaving his workstation?
Nothing, grabbing his drink should only take a few minutes and he will be right back
Signout of important websites and lock his computer
As long as he can keep his pc in his line of sight, nothing will go wrong.
Question 4 - Emily’s phone is going to run out of battery soon but as shes going to plug it in, she notices that the charging cable is frayed and has exposed wire. What should she do?
Charge her phone anyways since it is urgent and needs to be charged
Ask to borrow or purchase a new charger from a convenience store
Discard in a regular trash bin
Question 5 - What is a VPN?
A Virtual Private Network that encrypts your internet connection and hides your IP address
A type of antivirus software that protects your device from malware.
A secure Wi-Fi network provided by internet service providers for public use.
Question 6 - What is the difference between A White Hat hacker and a Black Hat hacker?
White hat is someone who hacks ethically in order to find weakness and improve security, black hat hackers are criminals who access confidential information for malicious purposes. (Correct answer)
White hat are criminals who access personal devices in order to steal bank information, while black hat hackers are criminals who black mail users through email scams
There is no difference, they are just titles that sound cool
Question 7 - Why should you have different passwords for different websites?
You shouldnt have different passwords because it is easier to remember the same password if you use many different websites.
It is easier for hackers and other criminals to access your information if they already have access to one of your accounts elsewhere (Correct answer)
Computers can only save a limited number of passwords online, leaving the rest up to you to remember
Question 8 - What are some indicators of a Phishing attempt in an email?
Typos throughout message, incorrect name addressing you, sender address not matching official company’s email. (Correct answer)
Attached files with vague names, attachments that are unrelated to the content of the email, links to external websites that you do not recognize
Requests for personal information to confirm account information, urgency or threatening tones.
Question 9 - Why is grounding important when handling the internal pieces of a computer?
To prevent electrostatic discharge from damaging electronic components.
It improves the performance of the components during installation.
It helps the computer recognize new hardware faster after installation.
Question 10 - What kind of cable will allow your pc to connect to Ethernet?
HDMI
LAN (Correct Answer
SATA
Question 11 - What should you do any time you use public networks?
Avoid using public networks generally, use VPN if you must access one, never view sensitive information on a public network or log in to accounts.
Turn off your firewall to avoid connection issues on public Wi-Fi.
Use the same trusted websites you normally use at home to stay safe
Question 12 - What is Cryptography?
Securing digital information by transforming it into unreadable data called ciphertext which is able to be decoded by another party with a matching key. (Correct answer)
A method of compressing data to reduce file size and improve storage efficiency.
A system used to back up data automatically across multiple servers.
Question 13 - Which of the following best describes the function of HTTPS in a URL?
It signifies that the data between your browser and the website is encrypted. (Correct answer)
. It means the website has been vetted by Google.
. It allows you to enter credit card info on any site safely

Question 14 - You are working in your office building and find a USB drive on your desk with a sticky note labeled, “Run program to update company database software” signed with your bosses first name. What should you do?
Do not plug in and report to your companies IT or security team immediately.
Plug in the device and run a windows scan to see if it contains anything harmful.
Plug in the device and run the programs it contains, since its from your boss
Question 15 - A threat actor is trying to find unused features in a commercial software package to bypass security controls. What type of vulnerability is this?
Misconfiguration
Cryptographic failure.
Insecure design. (Correct answer)
Submit Quiz
